rag_rules: When generating AGREE repairs, ensure that all identifiers used in assume/guarantee clauses are visible in the current component scope and properly declared.
Place range assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes only when concrete bounds or visible relations are provided.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or property references that are not present in the analyzed AADL model.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same AGREE annex for expressions.
Align helper declaration types with the types of the expressions they represent.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE clauses must follow the syntax: assume "label": expression; guarantee "label": expression; eq name : type = expression; const name : type = value; assign output = expression;
Component type contracts should not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL package identifiers or property sets that are not declared in the model's with clauses.
Do not assume that AADL property names like Compute_Execution_Time are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about missing packages or property sets
Implementation-level bindings and subcomponent port usage in type contracts
